title: Design & Supply Completes Management Buy-Out to Support Future Growth
excerpt: Design and Supply has completed a management buy-out, securing the long-term future of the business and continuing its commitment to UK manufacturing from South Wales. The business is now led by Tom Grother, Scott Davies and Damien Regis.
body:
A New Chapter for Design & Supply

Design and Supply is pleased to announce the successful completion of a management buy-out, securing the long-term future of the business and continuing its commitment to UK manufacturing from South Wales.

The business is now led by long-standing management team members Tom Grother, Scott Davies and Damien Regis – a team that has played a key role in the company's growth and day-to-day operations for many years.

For nearly 40 years, Design & Supply has manufactured specialist steel doorsets from its facility in Merthyr Tydfil, supplying projects across the commercial, public-sector, infrastructure and utilities sectors throughout the UK.

Today, the business manufactures a wide range of bespoke products including:

  • Fire-rated steel doors
  • Security-rated doorsets
  • Thermal and acoustic doors
  • Flood-resistant doors
  • Slimline architectural glazing systems

The management buy-out represents a continuation of the company's strong history of local leadership and long-term investment in both its people and manufacturing capabilities.

Director Tom Grother said:

“This is an exciting step forward for the business and something we're all incredibly proud of. Having been involved in Design & Supply for many years, we understand what makes the company successful — our people, our relationships, and our commitment to delivering high-quality products. We're proud to continue the legacy built over nearly four decades and remain committed to investing in the future of the business here in Wales.”

The transaction was supported by the Development Bank of Wales through funding from the Wales Business Succession Fund and Wales Flexible Investment Fund.

The new ownership team will now focus on continuing to strengthen Design & Supply's manufacturing capabilities, expanding its product offering, and supporting customers across increasingly complex and specialist projects.

As the business enters this next phase, Design & Supply remains committed to the values that have shaped the company over the last four decades — quality manufacturing, technical expertise, and long-term customer relationships.

title: Bespoke Steel Doorsets Supplied for a Landmark Welsh Sports Facility
excerpt: The FAW National Football Development Centre Extension at Hensol has been shortlisted for Commercial Development of the Year at the Insider Media Wales Property Awards 2026. Design & Supply supplied bespoke steel doorsets as part of the wider building package.
body:
The Football Association of Wales (FAW) National Football Development Centre Extension at Hensol has been shortlisted for Commercial Development of the Year at the Insider Media Wales Property Awards 2026 – recognising the project's contribution to the future of Welsh football infrastructure.

Photography acknowledgment: Sophie Jordan Photography.

Delivered by Willis Construction, the £3 million extension forms Phase Two of the FAW headquarters development and provides enhanced facilities for the Cymru National Teams, supporting elite-level preparation, training, and day-to-day operations.

The development includes:

  • Expanded gym and activation spaces
  • New player and staff facilities
  • Additional office accommodation
  • The Wynnstay 1876 Lecture Theatre for team briefings and analysis

Designed to create a high-performance environment, the project was delivered within a fully operational live site, with FAW staff and national teams continuing to use the facility throughout construction.

Design & Supply's Involvement

Design & Supply was proud to contribute to the project by supplying bespoke steel doorsets as part of the wider building package.

Projects of this nature require doorsets that not only provide long-term durability and reliable performance, but also integrate seamlessly within modern commercial environments where quality, functionality, and finish are all important considerations.

Working alongside the wider project team, our involvement supported the delivery of a facility designed to operate at the highest level of professional sport.

Supporting High-Performance Environments

Elite sports facilities demand durable, dependable building products capable of performing in high-use environments. Steel doorsets remain a key part of this, offering durability, security, and long-term reliability across commercial and public-sector applications.

The completed FAW National Football Development Centre Extension represents a significant investment in the future of Welsh football and provides a modern environment for players, staff, and national teams to prepare and perform.

We're proud to have played a part in such an important development and congratulate Willis Construction and the wider project team on the project's award shortlist recognition.

title: Car Park Fire Doors UK: A Complete Guide to Compliant Steel Doorsets
excerpt: In both residential and commercial developments, fire doors in car parks play a vital role in protecting escape routes and maintaining compartmentation. This guide covers the key challenges, compliance requirements and what to look for when specifying.
body:
Why Car Park Fire Doors Are Critical in UK Buildings

In both residential and commercial developments, fire doors in car parks play a vital role in protecting escape routes and maintaining compartmentation.

Correctly specified car park fire-rated doors help to:

  • Prevent the spread of fire
  • Contain smoke
  • Protect stairwells and escape routes
  • Support overall building fire strategy

All fire-rated steel doors in the UK must be tested to standards such as EN 1634-1 to demonstrate proven performance.

The Key Challenges for Car Park Fire Doors

High Usage Environments

Car park doors are used frequently, particularly in stair cores and access points. This demands robust, heavy-duty steel doors designed for continuous operation.

Exposure to Weather and Corrosion

Many external fire doors in UK car parks are exposed to rain, moisture, and pollutants. Without the right finish, doors can deteriorate quickly.

Strict Compliance Requirements

With increasing focus on building safety, UK fire door regulations require clear evidence of testing, certification, and correct specification.

What to Look for

When specifying steel fire doors for car parks in the UK, several key factors should be considered:

Fire-Rated Performance

Tested to EN 1634-1, ensuring reliable fire resistance and compliance.

Smoke Control

Many applications require fire and smoke doors, helping to maintain safe escape routes during a fire event.

Durability and Longevity

High-quality powder coated steel doors provide resistance to corrosion and wear, making them ideal for car park environments.

Security and Access Control

Modern developments often require secure fire-rated doors integrated with access control systems to manage entry points safely.

Application Versatility

A complete car park fire door solution should cover:

  • Stairwell fire doors
  • Car park entrance doors
  • Service and riser doors
  • Secure access doors

Why Early Specification Matters

Specifying car park doors in the UK early in a project helps to:

  • Ensure compliance with building regulations
  • Align with fire strategies and compartmentation plans
  • Avoid costly delays or redesigns
  • Guarantee compatibility with access control systems

Working with a specialist supplier ensures your steel fire doors are tailored to the specific demands of the project.

Steel Doors for Every Application

From multi-storey to underground doors, each environment requires a tailored approach.

At Design & Supply, we provide:

  • Fully compliant car park fire doors
  • Durable steel doorsets designed for high-traffic use
  • Solutions built for long-term performance in harsh environments

Need Help Specifying Car Park Fire Doors?

If you're working on a project and need guidance on car park doors, our team can help you specify the right solution.

We support contractors, architects, and developers with compliant, high-performance steel fire doors for car parks.

Discover our fire-rated doors here.

title: Design & Supply Strengthens Fire Door Certification UK Compliance by Joining UKTC ensure
excerpt: Design & Supply is the first steel door manufacturer to join UKTC ensure, a UKAS-accredited third-party certification scheme designed to strengthen fire door certification in the UK and support the principles of the Golden Thread.
body:
Design & Supply is pleased to announce that we are the first steel door manufacturer to join UKTC ensure, a UKAS-accredited third-party certification scheme designed to strengthen fire door certification in the UK and support the principles of the Golden Thread.

This step reinforces our commitment to delivering certified fire doors with clear, traceable evidence – helping specifiers, contractors, and building owners meet increasingly strict UK fire door certification requirements.

Fire Door Certification UK: What Is UKTC ensure?

UKTC ensure is the first UKAS-accredited third-party fire door certification scheme developed around the Golden Thread principles established by the Building Regulations Advisory Committee (BRAC).

Designed in collaboration with industry experts, manufacturers, and government guidance, UKTC ensure provides a robust, future-ready approach to fire door certification in the UK.

Following the Grenfell Inquiry, UKTC developed ensure to go beyond minimum compliance – delivering a transparent, auditable certification framework that supports safer buildings and clearer accountability across the supply chain.

Why Fire Door Certification Matters in the UK

Fire door certification in the UK is under increasing regulatory scrutiny, particularly for higher-risk buildings and regulated environments.

Third-party certification provides independent verification that a fire door:

  • Has been tested to recognised UK and European standards
  • Is manufactured consistently under controlled conditions
  • Continues to perform as declared over time

UKTC ensure strengthens this process by creating a single source of truth for certified fire doors, reducing ambiguity and improving confidence for all stakeholders.

What UKTC ensure Means for Design & Supply Fire Doors

By joining UKTC ensure, Design & Supply enhances the way we demonstrate fire door certification compliance in the UK for our fire-rated steel doorsets.

For our customers, this means:

  • UKAS-accredited third-party fire door certification
  • Clear, traceable certification evidence
  • Improved transparency across specification and procurement
  • Reduced compliance risk on regulated projects

Each certified doorset is supported by verified data hosted within the UKTC ensure vault, ensuring information remains accessible and auditable throughout the building lifecycle.

How UKTC ensure Supports Ongoing Fire Door Compliance

UKTC ensure follows a structured certification process designed to maintain long-term compliance:

Evaluation

Independent review of fire test data and assessment evidence to define certification scope.

Factory Production Control (FPC) Audit

Audits verify that fire doors are manufactured in line with certified standards and management systems.

Sampling & Testing

Products may be sampled for additional testing to confirm declared performance.

Certification & Digital Records

Certificates and supporting data are uploaded to the UKTC ensure vault, creating a centralised record.

Ongoing Surveillance

Annual audits and testing ensure continued compliance with UK fire door certification requirements.

Certified products feature QR-coded identification labels and are linked directly to verified certification records.

Supporting Safer Buildings Through Fire Door Certification UK

Joining UKTC ensure reflects Design & Supply's long-term commitment to fire door safety, certification, and regulatory compliance in the UK.

As building regulations continue to evolve, we remain focused on delivering UK-manufactured, certified fire-rated steel doorsets backed by robust third-party certification and full traceability.

Looking to specify certified fire doors in the UK?

Speak to our technical team about fire door certification, third-party testing, and compliant steel doorset solutions.

Find us on UKTC ensure here.

title: Insulated Doors Explained: What They Are, How They Work, and Why Thermal Performance Matters
excerpt: As energy efficiency standards continue to tighten, insulated doors are becoming a critical part of modern building design. This guide explains what they are, how they reduce heat loss, and why thermal performance matters when specifying steel doors.
body:
As energy efficiency standards continue to tighten, insulated doors are becoming a critical part of modern building design. Once viewed as a secondary consideration, thermal performance is now a core requirement for external doors across commercial, industrial, and public-sector projects.

In this guide, we explain what insulated doors are, how they reduce heat loss, and why thermal performance – measured by U-values – matters more than ever when specifying steel doors.

What Are Insulated Doors?

They are doorsets designed to reduce heat transfer between internal and external environments. Unlike traditional single-skin doors, insulated doors incorporate thermal insulation within the door leaf and frame to improve energy efficiency and limit heat loss.

In steel applications, insulated doors typically feature:

  • An insulated core material
  • Thermally optimised frames
  • High-performance seals

The result is a doorset that maintains the strength and durability of steel while delivering significantly improved thermal performance.

How Do They Reduce Heat Loss?

Heat loss through doors occurs when warm internal air transfers through poorly insulated materials or gaps around the doorset. Insulated doors combat this through a combination of design features working together.

Key elements include:

  • Insulated cores that limit heat transfer
  • Thermal breaks to prevent cold bridging
  • Precision engineering for tighter tolerances
  • Robust sealing systems around the perimeter

When correctly designed, an insulated steel doorset significantly reduces heat loss compared to older or uninsulated steel doors.

Understanding U-Values

The thermal performance of insulated doors is measured using a U-value. This indicates how much heat passes through a building element.

The lower the U-value, the better the insulation

For insulated doors, the U-value reflects the performance of the entire doorset, including:

  • Door leaf
  • Frame
  • Glazing (if applicable)
  • Seals

Modern insulated steel doors can achieve U-values far lower than traditional steel solutions, making them suitable for projects where energy efficiency is a priority.

Part L of the Building Regulations

In the UK, the thermal performance of external doors is governed by Part L of the Building Regulations, which aims to reduce energy consumption and heat loss in buildings.

Current Part L requirements set maximum U-values depending on the application:

  • New-build external doors: up to 1.6 W/m²K
  • Predominantly glazed doors: up to 1.4 W/m²K or minimum Doorset Energy Rating of Band C
  • Replacement external fire doors: up to 1.8 W/m²K

Specifying insulated doors with strong thermal performance helps ensure compliance – not just today, but as standards continue to evolve.

Where Are They Commonly Used?

Thanks to their improved thermal efficiency, insulated doors are now widely specified across a range of sectors, including:

  • Commercial buildings
  • Industrial units and warehouses
  • Schools and educational facilities
  • Healthcare and public-sector buildings
  • Refurbishment and retrofit projects

In these environments, insulated steel doors offer a balance of durability, performance, and compliance.

Why Insulated Steel Doors Are the Preferred Choice

Modern insulated steel doorsets deliver:

  • Excellent thermal performance
  • Long-term durability
  • Resistance to impact and wear
  • Suitability for demanding environments

This makes insulated steel doors an ideal solution where performance, longevity, and energy efficiency must be achieved together.

A New Generation of Insulated Steel Doorsets

Advances in design and manufacturing have led to a new generation of insulated steel doorsets that go beyond minimum regulatory thresholds.

At Design & Supply, this approach is reflected in Therma-Line a range of thermally efficient steel doorsets engineered to achieve U-values as low as 1.3 W/m²K.

Rather than designing to minimum requirements, modern insulated doorsets like these provide valuable performance headroom, helping future-proof projects as regulations continue to tighten.

All Therma-Line doorsets are:

  • Manufactured in the UK
  • Bespoke to suit project requirements
  • Designed for both new-build and refurbishment applications

Choosing the Right Insulated Door

When specifying insulated doors, it's important to look beyond a single figure. Key considerations include:

  • Whole-door U-value
  • Build quality and durability
  • Suitability for the building type
  • Manufacturer support and technical expertise

A well-specified insulated steel doorset can improve building performance, support compliance, and deliver long-term value.

Insulated Doors for Modern Building Standards

As thermal performance becomes a central focus in building design, insulated doors are no longer optional – they are essential. With the right specification, insulated steel doors can deliver energy efficiency without compromising on strength, durability, or compliance.

If you're specifying insulated doors where thermal performance matters, working with a specialist manufacturer like Design & Supply can help ensure confidence from design through to delivery.

title: U-Values Explained: What They Mean for Steel Doors and Part L Compliance
excerpt: When specifying external doors for commercial, industrial or public-sector buildings, thermal performance is now a critical consideration. This guide explains what U-values are, why they matter for steel doors, and how they relate to Part L of the Building Regulations.
body:
Thermal Performance in Steel Doorsets

When specifying external doors for commercial, industrial, or public-sector buildings, thermal performance is now a critical consideration. One of the key measures used to assess this performance is the U-value.

In this guide, we explain what U-values are, why they matter for steel doors, and how they relate to Part L of the Building Regulations.

What Is a U-Value?

A U-value measures how much heat passes through a building element, such as a wall, window, or door. It is expressed in W/m²K (watts per square metre per degree Kelvin).

In simple terms:

  • Lower U-value = better thermal insulation
  • Higher U-value = more heat loss

For external doors, the U-value indicates how effectively the doorset prevents heat from escaping the building.

Why U-Values Matter for Steel Doors

Steel doors are widely used in commercial and public-sector buildings due to their strength, security, fire performance, and durability. However, traditional steel doors were not always designed with thermal efficiency as a priority.

As energy standards have tightened, steel doors must now balance:

  • Structural strength
  • Longevity and resistance to damage
  • Thermal performance

A well-designed thermally insulated steel doorset can significantly reduce heat loss, improve overall building efficiency, and support compliance with current regulations.

Part L of the Building Regulations Explained

Part L of the Building Regulations focuses on the conservation of fuel and power. It sets minimum energy performance requirements for building elements, including external doors.

U-Value Requirements Under Part L

For steel doors, Part L sets different thresholds depending on the application:

  • New dwellings:
    • All external doors, including glazed doors, must have a maximum U-Value of 1.6 W/m²K.
    • For new-builds where doors are mostly glazed:
      • Maximum U-Value of 1.4 W/m²K, or
      • Minimum Doorset Energy Rating of Band C.
  • Replacement doors in existing buildings:
    • Different U-Value thresholds apply depending on the project.
  • External fire doorsets (as defined in Approved Document B):
    • Maximum permitted U-Value of 1.8 W/m²K.

These limits ensure that external doors do not undermine the overall thermal performance of the building envelope.

U-Values for New Dwellings

In new residential buildings, Part L places further emphasis on thermal performance. For new dwellings, doors (including glazed doors) are typically required to achieve U-values around 1.6 W/m²K, depending on the overall building design and compliance approach.

This highlights how increasingly important door performance has become within modern energy-efficient construction.

How Steel Doors Can Achieve Low U-Values

Achieving a low U-value in a steel doorset requires careful engineering. Key factors include:

  • Thermally broken door leaf and frame construction
  • High-performance insulation materials
  • Robust perimeter sealing systems
  • Precision manufacturing and installation

When these elements are correctly designed and integrated, steel doors can deliver strong thermal performance without compromising their inherent benefits.

Why Low U-Values Benefit Building Owners and Specifiers

Specifying steel doors with low U-values offers several advantages:

  • Improved energy efficiency
  • Reduced heat loss and energy costs
  • Easier compliance with Part L requirements
  • Increased comfort for building occupants
  • Future-proofing against tightening regulations

For commercial and public-sector projects, these benefits are increasingly important during planning, approval, and long-term operation.

U-Values and Real-World Performance

It's important to note that U-values are influenced by:

  • Door size and configuration
  • Hardware selection
  • Glazing type (where applicable)
  • Installation quality

As a result, U-values are often based on tested or calculated performance within defined tolerances. Accurate specification and professional installation are essential to achieving the intended thermal results.

Steel Doors Designed for Modern Energy Standards

Today's steel doorsets are no longer just about security and fire performance. Thermal efficiency is now a core requirement.

By understanding U-values and how they relate to Part L, specifiers and contractors can make informed decisions that balance compliance, performance, and durability.

If you're specifying steel doors for a project where energy efficiency matters, understanding U-values is the first step toward achieving a compliant, high-performance solution.

Find Out More About Therma-Line

For further information on Therma-Line thermal steel doorsets, including technical specifications and suitability for your project:

  • Visit our product page
  • Download the datasheet
  • Contact the Design & Supply team

Our specialists are available to provide guidance from early specification through to manufacture and delivery.

Read more about Part L of the Building Regulations here

title: Design & Supply Launches New Range of Thermal Steel Doorsets with a U-Value as Low as 1.3
excerpt: Design & Supply has launched Therma-Line, a new range of high-performance thermal steel doorsets achieving U-Values as low as 1.3 W/m²K. The range supports Part L compliance across new-build and refurbishment projects.
body:
Design & Supply is proud to announce the launch of Therma-Line, a new range of high-performance thermal steel doorsets developed to meet rising demands for energy efficiency without compromising strength, durability, or compliance.

With thermal performance achieving U-Values as low as 1.3 W/m²K, Therma-Line doorsets support compliance with Part L of the Building Regulations. As a result, they are suitable for both new-build and refurbishment projects across commercial, industrial, and public-sector environments.

Designed to Exceed Part L Requirements

Energy efficiency is now a key consideration in modern building design, and thermal performance plays an increasingly important role in external door specification. Part L of the Building Regulations sets clear limits on U-Values to help reduce heat loss and improve overall building performance.

For new dwellings, external doors (including glazed doors) must achieve a maximum U-Value of 1.6 W/m²K. In new-build applications where external doors are predominantly glazed, the requirement becomes more stringent, with a maximum permitted U-Value of 1.4 W/m²K or a minimum Doorset Energy Rating of Band C.

Different thresholds apply to replacement doors in existing buildings. For external fire doorsets, as defined in Approved Document B, a maximum U-Value of 1.8 W/m²K is permitted.

With thermal performance achieving U-Values as low as 1.3 W/m²K, Therma-Line steel doorsets not only meet these requirements but exceed them. This gives specifiers and contractors confidence that Part L compliance can be achieved comfortably as standards continue to evolve.

Rather than designing to minimum thresholds, Therma-Line provides additional performance headroom – helping future-proof projects while maintaining the strength, durability, and compliance expected from a steel doorset.

High Thermal Performance, Built in Steel

Many thermally efficient doorsets rely on alternative materials to achieve low U-Values. By contrast, Therma-Line doorsets are fabricated in steel, delivering superior strength, durability, and resistance to damage.

Advanced insulation, precision engineering, and robust sealing systems work together to minimise heat loss. At the same time, the doorset retains all the inherent benefits expected from a high-performance steel solution.

This makes Therma-Line suitable for projects where thermal performance, durability, and compliance must be achieved together.

Suitable for a Wide Range of Applications

Thanks to their enhanced thermal performance, Therma-Line doorsets are ideal for:

  • New-build developments requiring Part L compliance
  • Refurbishment and replacement projects in existing buildings
  • Commercial and industrial premises
  • Public-sector buildings where durability and energy efficiency are essential

In addition, all Therma-Line doorsets are manufactured in the UK. This ensures consistent quality, reliable lead times, and full technical support throughout the specification process.

Supporting Compliance Without Compromise

The launch of Therma-Line reinforces Design & Supply's commitment to developing future-ready steel doorsets. These products respond directly to evolving building regulations while maintaining the performance standards expected from a specialist manufacturer.

Crucially, specifiers no longer need to choose between thermal efficiency and structural integrity. Therma-Line delivers both in a single, fully engineered solution.

Find Out More About Therma-Line

For further information on Therma-Line thermal steel doorsets:

  • Visit our product page
  • Download the datasheet
  • Contact the Design & Supply team

Our specialists are available to provide guidance from early specification through to manufacture and delivery.

Read more about Part L of the Building Regulation's here: Approved Document L Volume 1, 2021 edition, page 23

title: Steel Flood Doors: The Complete 2025 Guide to Protecting UK Buildings from Rising Flood Risks
excerpt: The past year has brought some of the UK's wettest months on record, and flooding is becoming more common and more destructive. This guide explains why steel flood doors are now essential, how they work, and what to consider when specifying one.
body:
The past year has brought some of the UK's wettest months on record. From swollen rivers to sudden flash flooding, many parts of the country have been hit repeatedly by severe weather. As climate change continues to accelerate, heavy rainfall and storm-driven flooding are becoming far more common — and far more destructive.

For businesses, utilities and industrial sites, the impact can be enormous. Once water enters a building, the damage to equipment, stock, electrics and structure can run into the tens or hundreds of thousands. As a result, more organisations are now investing in robust flood-resilience measures, including specialist steel flood doors designed to keep water out during extreme weather.

Why Flood Doors Are Becoming Essential

Traditional steel doors are not designed to hold back rising water. Under pressure, water can seep through thresholds, frame joints and hardware points. Flood-rated doors, on the other hand, are built specifically to prevent water ingress during both static and fast-moving flood events.

Even sites that previously sat outside recognised flood zones are now experiencing issues due to:

  • Heavier, longer periods of rainfall
  • Groundwater saturation
  • Blocked drainage systems
  • Rapid surface-water flooding
  • Overflowing rivers and streams

With more unpredictable, high-intensity storms expected in the coming years, flood protection has become a priority for building managers and specifiers across the UK.

What Makes a Steel Flood Door Different?

Flood-resistant steel doors use a combination of reinforced construction and high-performance sealing systems to create a water-tight barrier. Each component plays a part, including:

  • Heavy-gauge steel door leaf and frame
  • Compression seals designed specifically for flood pressure
  • Reinforced hinges and locking points
  • Precision-engineered thresholds
  • Flood-rated hardware and fixings

Together, these features prevent water from forcing its way around — or through — the door during a flooding event.

900mm Flood Rating: Tested Performance You Can Rely On

Design & Supply's flood-rated steel doors are engineered and tested to withstand water depths of up to 900mm. This makes them suitable for the vast majority of commercial and industrial flood-risk scenarios in the UK.

A 900mm rating means:

  • The door can hold back significant water pressure
  • All seals remain tight and effective during prolonged exposure
  • The frame and leaf maintain structural integrity
  • Water cannot breach the door under standard testing conditions

This level of performance is particularly important for critical infrastructure, plant rooms, electrical areas and buildings storing valuable machinery or stock.

Professional Flood Testing During Installation — And Every Year After

To ensure every flood door performs exactly as designed, we carry out flood testing as part of the installation process. This allows us to verify that:

  • All seals are correctly seated
  • Thresholds are fully watertight
  • Frames are secure and free from gaps
  • Hardware is correctly torqued and aligned

This initial test gives clients complete confidence that the door is flood-ready from day one.

We also offer annual flood testing visits, where our engineers return to site to:

  • Recheck seals and compression
  • Inspect frame integrity
  • Test water resistance under controlled conditions
  • Identify early signs of wear or deterioration

Regular testing is the most reliable way to maintain long-term protection and ensure the door continues to meet its original flood-resistance rating.

Where Flood Doors Are Most Commonly Used

Steel flood doors are installed across a wide range of sectors, including:

  • Utility and pumping stations
  • Warehouses and logistics hubs
  • Coastal and riverside buildings
  • Transport and rail infrastructure
  • Water treatment facilities
  • Substations and plant rooms
  • Data and communications centers
  • Public-sector and municipal buildings

Any site where flooding could cause major operational disruption will benefit from a reliable, tested flood barrier.

Key Features of Design & Supply Flood-Rated Doors

  • 900mm flood rating for strong, dependable protection
  • Robust steel construction for long-lasting durability
  • High-compression sealing system to stop water ingress
  • Optional security upgrades, including SR-rated configurations
  • Made to order, ensuring a perfect fit for new openings or retrofits
  • Available in single or double leaf designs
  • Suitable for repeated flood cycles
  • Professional installation flood testing + annual retesting available

The Cost of Flood Damage

Flooding remains one of the most financially damaging events a UK business can face. Losses often include:

  • Electrical system failure
  • Equipment and stock damage
  • Structural degradation
  • Contamination and mould
  • Long-term operational downtime
  • Increased insurance premiums

In many cases, the cost of one flood far exceeds the investment in appropriate flood-resilient door systems.

Choosing the Right Flood Door

When specifying a flood door, consider:

  • Required water depth rating
  • Whether the site faces static or fast-moving floodwater
  • How frequently the area has experienced flooding
  • Security requirements
  • Any fire-rating requirements
  • Surrounding wall/floor construction and sealing needs

Our technical team is available to help with design, specification and installation guidance.

Why Work With Design & Supply?

With more than 35 years of specialist manufacturing experience, Design & Supply has become a trusted supplier of high-performance steel doors across the UK. Our flood-rated doors are built to withstand demanding environments and are already used on some of the country's most sensitive and high-risk sites.

We offer:

  • Industry-leading 900mm flood protection
  • Full installation flood testing
  • Annual on-site flood testing services
  • Expert technical support
  • Bespoke manufacturing
  • Fast lead times
  • Proven reliability across multiple sectors

title: What is LPS 1175? A Complete Guide to Security Ratings for Steel Doors
excerpt: LPS 1175 is the leading security standard used across the UK to measure and certify the intrusion resistance of doors, shutters and enclosures. This guide breaks down what it is, how the testing works, and what the SR ratings really mean.
body:
When choosing a security door, one of the most important factors to consider is how well it protects against forced entry. That's where LPS 1175 comes in – the leading security standard used across the UK to measure and certify the intrusion resistance of doors, shutters, enclosures, and other physical security products.

Design and Supply manufacture LPS 1175 certified steel doors up to SR4, giving our customers independently tested protection for a wide range of projects – from schools and retail units to utilities, commercial buildings, and high-risk sites.

This guide breaks down what LPS 1175 is, how the testing works, and what the security ratings (SR levels) really mean.

What Does LPS 1175 Mean?

LPS stands for Loss Prevention Standard, and LPS 1175 is published by the Loss Prevention Certification Board (LPCB) – the UK's leading authority on security product testing and certification.

In simple terms, LPS 1175 determines how long a product can delay a real intruder using known toolsets.

What Does LPS 1175 Measure?

LPS 1175 measures how effectively a product can delay an attacker until a security response arrives.

This is done using:

  • A defined toolkit representing what intruders are likely to use
  • A set amount of attack time depending on the security rating
  • A controlled test environment that simulates real-world break-in attempts and ideal conditions for a burglar.

The result is a security rating known as an SR level.

Understanding SR Levels (SR1–SR4)

Each SR level represents a different threat scenario, attack capability, and level of determination.

Here's a clear breakdown:

Max attack time refers to the committed time the technician can physically attack the product in a specified location.

Total test time includes breaks – simulating the real behaviour of intruders who may pause, re-position, or switch tools.

As the SR level increases, so does the tool severity, skill level, and determination of the attacker being simulated.

How LPS 1175 Testing Works

Each steel door is installed into a realistic substrate such as:

  • Timber
  • Brickwork
  • Concrete block
  • Steelwork

This ensures testing reflects real-life conditions rather than a laboratory setup.

An LPCB engineer then attempts to break through the door using the designated tools for that SR level. Tools may include:

  • Screwdrivers
  • Chisels
  • Pliers
  • Bolt cutters
  • Crowbars
  • Drills (higher SR levels)
  • Sledgehammer (SR4+)

The door must continue to prevent access for the full attack duration to pass.

Why LPS 1175 Matters

When specifying a steel door, LPS 1175 certification gives you:

Measurable, independent proof of security performance

You know exactly what the door can withstand and for how long.

Confidence that the product is tested under realistic conditions

The testing simulates how prepared intruders behave, not theoretical calculations.

A security door that meets industry and insurance requirements

LPS 1175 is widely recognised across construction, utilities, government, and education.

Clear guidance on the right door for each risk level

Whether you need protection from opportunistic theft or high-risk targeted attacks, there's an SR level that matches the threat.

Design and Supply Steel Doors – Certified up to SR4

At Design and Supply, we manufacture steel doors tested and certified to LPS 1175, giving you security options for:

  • Residential and commercial properties (SR1 / SR2)
  • Utilities, substations, and critical infrastructure (SR2 / SR3)
  • High-risk environments requiring advanced protection (SR4)

Every door is tested, certified, and built to perform where it matters most.

If you're unsure which SR rating your project requires, our expert team can help you select the right solution.

Speak to Our Team

Want to learn more about LPS 1175 or discuss a project? Our technical specialists are here to help.

Contact us

title: Design and Supply Achieves Green Growth and Equality Pledges
excerpt: Design and Supply has achieved both the Green Growth Pledge and the Equality Pledge, marking a significant commitment to sustainability and inclusivity within its operations.
body:
Design and Supply proudly announces its achievement of both the Green Growth Pledge and the Equality Pledge, marking a significant commitment to sustainability and inclusivity within its operations.

Under the Green Growth Pledge, Design and Supply has committed to stringent environmental practices, including monitoring and managing energy and water consumption, implementing the waste hierarchy for recycling, and exploring renewable energy options. The company aims to reduce its environmental footprint while supporting local suppliers and investing in the local economy.

Simultaneously, through the Equality Pledge, Design and Supply upholds a firm stance against discrimination in all forms. The company ensures equal opportunities for all employees, regardless of age, disability, gender identity, race, religion, or sexual orientation. This commitment extends to creating a supportive and inclusive work environment where diversity is valued and respected.

Design and Supply reaffirms its commitment to upholding these pledges through regular reviews and continuous improvement, ensuring that both environmental sustainability and equality remain core values driving its operations.

title: Temple Quarter Project Shortlisted for Schüco Excellence Award
excerpt: The Research Hub has been shortlisted for the Schüco Excellence Awards 2025. The project transformed two Victorian industrial buildings into a state-of-the-art research hub for the University of Bristol.
body:
Research Hub has been shortlisted for the Schüco Excellence Awards 2025.

This unique project breathed new life into two Victorian industrial buildings, the Retort House and Coal Shed, transforming them into a state-of-the-art research hub for the University of Bristol. Our role involved delivering a technically complex, bespoke glazing package that met stringent modern standards while preserving the building's industrial heritage.

From curved arched screens fabricated to match 19th-century stonework to modern high-performance glazed partitions inside, every detail was designed and engineered for performance, precision, and beauty.

Read the full case study and explore how the project came to life: Read more here

title: Design and Supply Secures Ebbw Vale Multi-Storey Car Park with Enhanced Anti-Vandalism Security Grilles
excerpt: Design and Supply has completed the installation of enhanced anti-vandalism security grilles at the Ebbw Vale multi-storey car park, in collaboration with Blaenau Gwent County Borough Council.
body:
Design and Supply is proud to announce the successful installation of enhanced anti-vandalism security grilles at the Ebbw Vale multi-storey car park, a vital infrastructure project undertaken in collaboration with Blaenau Gwent County Borough Council.

This project highlights Design and Supply's commitment to delivering tailored, high-quality security solutions. The newly installed grilles have been specifically designed to address the challenges of a high-traffic environment, providing long-term durability and effective deterrence against vandalism.

Customised Security for Public Spaces

The installed grilles feature:

  • Exceptional Durability: Constructed to withstand demanding environments and resist damage.
  • Advanced Security: A robust barrier to prevent unauthorised access and protect vital infrastructure.
  • Tailored Design: Bespoke solutions crafted to meet the specific requirements of the car park.

The installation was completed efficiently by Design and Supply's expert team, ensuring minimal disruption while upholding the highest standards of quality and craftsmanship.

Director, Chris Weed, commented “We're delighted to have partnered with Blaenau Gwent County Borough Council on this important project. At Design and Supply our enhanced security grilles are designed to deliver not only robust protection but also peace of mind for those who use and manage the facility.”

title: Design and Supply Achieves Remarkable Success in Recent Fire Testing
excerpt: Design and Supply's new fire door exceeded expectations in recent fire testing, performing throughout an extended 4-hour test against an initial 2-hour evaluation.
body:
News Release: Design and Supply Achieves Remarkable Success in Recent Fire Testing

Design and Supply – a leading provider of high-quality steel and glazed doors. We are delighted to announce the outstanding success of our recent fire testing program. The unwavering commitment of our entire team, spearheaded by our newly appointed Director Scott Davies, from meticulous design to manufacturing, was instrumental in surpassing even our most optimistic expectations.

The fire test, designed to assess the durability and resilience of Design and Supply's new fire door, was initially set for a 2-hour evaluation. However, the door exceeded all anticipations by showcasing exceptional performance throughout an extended 4-hour test. This remarkable achievement is a testament to the company's commitment to delivering products that go above and beyond industry standards.

During the rigorous testing process, the doors displayed impressive levels of resilience, with no signs of failure observed. This success underscores the quality, precision, and craftsmanship embedded in the design and manufacturing processes at Design and Supply.

“Our team at Design and Supply is thrilled to celebrate the success of this fire testing program,” declared Chris Weed, Director at Design and Supply. “This achievement is a testament to the collective effort, expertise, and unwavering dedication of our team members, who are committed to delivering doors of the highest quality and safety standards to our customers.”

The results of the fire testing program showcase Design and Supply's continuous pursuit of excellence in product development. The doors' ability to withstand a 4-hour fire test positions us as a reliable and resilient choice for customers seeking top-tier fire-rated solutions.

As Design and Supply celebrates this achievement, the company remains dedicated to pushing the boundaries of innovation and ensuring the safety and satisfaction of our valued customers.

title: Design and Supply Employee Champions Team
excerpt: Design and Supply formed the Employee Champions Team to ensure the voices of its employees are not just heard but acted upon, with members drawn from diverse departments across the organisation.
body:
Amplifying Voices, Fostering a Thriving Work Environment

Earlier this year, Design and Supply took a bold step towards enriching the lives of its valued employees by forming the Employee Champions Team. This team is comprised of dedicated individuals from diverse departments across the organisation, united by a common purpose: to ensure that the voices of our employees are not just heard but acted upon.

At Design and Supply, we understand that our people are at the heart of our success. Our commitment to nurturing a thriving work environment, where every member of our team feels their values are appreciated, is unwavering. The Employee Champions Team embodies this commitment, serving as the catalyst for positive change within the organisation.

Since its inception, the Employee Champions Team has been an incredible force for good. They have listened attentively to the concerns, suggestions, and aspirations of our employees and translated them into meaningful actions. From addressing requests for new chilled water fountains to the implementation of a company-wide monthly newsletter, the team has been proactive and effective in creating positive changes.

One of the most notable achievements of the Employee Champions Team has been the introduction of a unique birthday incentive. Now, our staff members have the opportunity to spin the birthday wheel and win exciting prizes on their special day. This innovative initiative not only adds an element of fun to our work environment but also underscores our commitment to recognising and celebrating our employees' individuality.

The work that the Employee Champions Team is carrying out has exceeded expectations, and we express our deepest gratitude to the entire team for their dedication and proactive efforts. As Design and Supply moves forward, we remain committed to fostering a workplace where everyone's contributions are valued, voices are heard, and actions are taken to ensure a thriving and inclusive work environment.

title: Introducing Our New Directors at Design and Supply
excerpt: Design and Supply has appointed Scott Davies and Damian Regis as Directors on its Board, recognising two long-standing members of the team.
body:
Design and Supply, a leading name in the world of steel doors and architectural solutions, is thrilled to announce the appointment of Scott Davies and Damian Regis as Directors on its esteemed Board.

Scott Davies, an integral part of the Design and Supply family for over 22 years, embarked on his journey with the company as an apprentice programmer for laser cutting machines. Over the years, Scott has held various pivotal roles within our organisation, spanning quoting, procurement, and planning. Five years into his tenure, he transitioned into the realm of designing and programming steel doors.

During this phase of his career, Scott played a ground-breaking role in the development of our Slimline door range. From conceiving early-stage designs to research, development, pricing, and estimating, he was instrumental in creating a product. In his role as a Director, Scott envisions steering sustainable growth, strengthening brand recognition, and enhancing employee engagement. His sights are also set on inspiring future generations to explore the world of manufacturing. Scott's ascent through the ranks exemplifies our enduring commitment to nurturing talent from within.

Damian Regis, with an extensive background of over 25 years in the manufacturing industry, brings a wealth of global experience from his time in Tier 1 automotive manufacturing. His journey has taken him around the world, but his heart led him back to his hometown of Merthyr Tydfil, where he joined Design and Supply as Operations Manager seven years ago. In this role, Damian championed growth and improvement across our business.

Leveraging his vast experience, Damian focused on driving innovation and efficiency, reshaping our operations and strategies. His expertise perfectly complements and enhances our existing leadership team. Damian's enthusiasm for achieving remarkable growth aligns seamlessly with the Board's vision of positioning Design and Supply as the leading manufacturers in our industry.

With Scott and Damian stepping into their new roles, we eagerly anticipate the positive impact their leadership will have on our business. The entire Board is excited about the new horizons that lie ahead and firmly believes that their insights and contributions will fuel our continued success. We invite you to join us in congratulating Scott and Damian on their well-deserved appointments. Their accomplishments are a testament to the exceptional talent that resides within our organisation.
